Package org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise

Class OutgoingEmail

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise.SObject
org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise.OutgoingEmail
public class OutgoingEmail extends SObject

Java class for OutgoingEmail complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="OutgoingEmail">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{urn:sobject.enterprise.soap.sforce.com}sObject">
       <sequence>
         <element name="BccAddress" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="BccIds" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="CcAddress" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="CcIds" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ContentDocumentIds" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ContentDocumentLinks" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}QueryResult" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="EmailTemplate" type="{urn:sobject.enterprise.soap.sforce.com}sObject" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="EmailTemplateId" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ExternalId" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="HtmlBody" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="InReplyTo" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="MessageId" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="OutgoingEmailRelations" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}QueryResult" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="References" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="RelatedTo" type="{urn:sobject.enterprise.soap.sforce.com}sObject" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="RelatedToId" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="Subject" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="TextBody" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ToAddress" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ToIds" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="ValidatedFromAddress" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="Who" type="{urn:sobject.enterprise.soap.sforce.com}sObject" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="WhoId" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ID" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getBccAddress

      public String getBccAddress()
      Gets the value of the bccAddress property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setBccAddress

      public void setBccAddress(String value)
      Sets the value of the bccAddress property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getBccIds

      public List<String> getBccIds()
      Gets the value of the bccIds property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the bccIds property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBccIds().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the bccIds property.

      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • getCcIds

      public List<String> getCcIds()
      Gets the value of the ccIds property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the ccIds property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCcIds().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the ccIds property.

      public List<String> getContentDocumentIds()
      Gets the value of the contentDocumentIds property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the contentDocumentIds property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getContentDocumentIds().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the contentDocumentIds property.

    • getToIds

      public List<String> getToIds()
      Gets the value of the toIds property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the toIds property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getToIds().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the toIds property.
